channel.title: NYT > World > Middle East
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe, Eric Schmidt, Helene Cooper and Yan Zhuang

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Saturday
item.description: The U.S. military’s search-and-rescue operation for a missing American airman entered its second day. The Israeli military struck a major petrochemical complex in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: The site of a strike at Shahid Beheshti University in Tehran on Saturday.

item.title: Israel Strikes Iran’s Largest Petrochemical Complex
item.description: The sprawling complex in the city of Mahshahr was taken offline after strikes hit two utility plants that provided basic services like gas and power, Iranian officials said.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Mahshahr petrochemical complex in 2011.

item.title: Satellite image company restricts Mideast content, citing a U.S. government request.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.media:credit: Planet Labs PBC, via Associated Press
item.media:description: A satellite photo of Kharg Island in Iran in February that was distributed by Planet Labs.

item.title: Here’s how two airmen would have ejected from the F-15E.
item.description: A step-by-step look at the process by which the two crew members probably ejected from the fighter jet.
item.dc:creator: Mika Gröndahl, Lily Boyce and Thomas Gibbons-Neff

item.title: U.S. Fighter Jet Downed Over Iran Was Probably Based at R.A.F. Lakenheath, U.K. Airfield
item.description: A military analyst identified markings consistent with a squadron based at R.A.F. Lakenheath, one of two British bases that host the largest U.S. fighter jet operation in Europe.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: A fighter jet at R.A.F. Lakenheath in Mildenhall, England, in January.

item.title: A projectile hit near Iran’s Bushehr nuclear plant, killing a worker.
item.description: There was no reported increase in radiation levels. A string of recent attacks near the Persian Gulf facility has fueled fears of a nuclear accident.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.media:description: A satellite image of the nuclear power plant in Bushehr, Iran, in December.

item.title: Missing Airman Raises Concerns That Iran Could Gain Leverage Over the U.S.
item.description: Since 1979, Iran has repeatedly used Americans and Europeans detained on its territory to win concessions over more powerful adversaries.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Women walking by a mural of the Statue of Liberty on the outer wall of the former U.S. Embassy in Tehran. In 1979, a group of militant students took over the embassy and kept 52 Americans captive for 444 days.

item.title: Trump Pledged a Quick End to the Iran War, but He Hasn’t Explained How
item.description: President Trump bet that American firepower could cow Iran into compliance. So far, Iran’s leaders have been unwilling to quit.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Neither President Trump nor Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has been able to explain how the war, now in its fifth week, will end.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe, Eric Schmitt and Helene Cooper

item.title: ‘Iran Is Being Destroyed in Front of Our Eyes’: Tehran Is Gripped by Fear
item.description: Fifteen residents of Tehran said in telephone interviews and text messages that the capital was weathering heavy bombardment.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Residents in front of buildings damaged by airstrikes in Karaj, Iran, on Friday.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Friday
item.description: An American fighter jet was shot down by Iran for the first time since the start of the war, leading to an urgent search-and-rescue operation for a missing crew member.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Amit Elkayam for The New York Times
item.media:description: A home in Ramat Gan, Israel, damaged by a projectile during an Iranian missile strike on Friday.

item.title: Ex-US Army Pilot Recalls Battle for Survival When Shot Down in Iraq
item.description: Ronald Young Jr. was in the Army when his Apache Longbow copter went down during the U.S. invasion of Iraq.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Ronald Young Jr. boarding a transport plane near Baghdad in 2003, after escaping captivity.

item.title: Iran’s Defenses Have Been Struck, but They Can Still Fire Missiles and Drones
item.description: Iran has sent waves of missiles and drones at Israel and Gulf states in recent weeks and shot down an American fighter jet on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Kulish
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Planet Labs Pbc/Planet Labs PBC, via Associated Press
item.media:description: This satellite photo shows damage after an Iranian attack at Al Udeid Air Base in Qatar last month. Iran struck the regional air headquarters of U.S. Central Command, damaging an early-warning radar system.

item.title: Iran Shoots Down U.S. Fighter Jet, Setting Off American Search and Rescue
item.description: One crew member was rescued. The risky operation to find the second airman came as the United States and Israel struck infrastructure targets in Iran, prompting Iranian retaliation in the Gulf.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Helene Cooper and Thomas Fuller
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: What We Know About the F-15E Strike Eagle Shot Down by Iran
item.description: An F-15E Strike Eagle was shot down over Iran and an A-10 Warthog crashed near the Strait of Hormuz, officials said on Friday. Two airmen were rescued, and one is unaccounted for.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor and Aurelien Breeden
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: An F-15E Strike Eagle, the type of plane shot down in Iran.

item.title: Iran Is Quickly Repairing Missile Bunkers, U.S. Intelligence Says
item.description: Reports cast doubt on how close the United States is to destroying Iran’s missile capability, a key goal in the war.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: The tail section of a missile in Israel’s northern Jordan Valley on Friday. Iran has continued to strike at the country, even with its reduced arsenal and more careful use of launchers.

item.title: The F-15E Strike Eagle: What to Know
item.description: The U.S. military aircraft with a two-person crew was shot down in Iran on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: The U.S. aircraft that was brought down over Iran has been identified as an F-15E Strike Eagle. One of its two-member crew is still missing.

item.title: Hegseth Says U.S. Troops Are Fighting for Jesus. The Pope Disagrees.
item.description: In sharp contrast to the Trump administration’s calls for Christian prayers for the war effort, Pope Leo XIV says military domination is “entirely foreign to the way of Jesus Christ.”
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Pope Leo, during the Holy Thursday Mass in Rome, said the Christian mission had often been “distorted by a desire for domination.”

item.title: What U.S. military Pilots Are Trained to Do if Shot Down
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: American military pilots are trained in a set of principles called Survival, Evasion, Resistance and Escape to prepare for the possibility of being shot down in hostile territory.

item.title: Iranian media outlets say helicopters are searching for the crew of the downed jet.
item.dc:creator: Leily Nikounazar and Yeganeh Torbati

item.title: Israel plans to seize control of parts of southern Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Christina Goldbaum, Samuel Granados and Sanjana Varghese

item.title: Kuwait Blames Iran for Attack on Power and Desalination Plants
item.description: In Kuwait, an Iranian attack on Friday damaged a power and water desalination plant, officials said. Both sides in the Iran war have ramped up strikes on civilian targets.
item.dc:creator: Aurelien Breeden
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Smoke rising from Kuwait’s international airport after a reported drone strike on Wednesday.

item.title: At War With Iran Again, Some Israelis Fear Conflict Is Becoming Routine
item.description: A majority of Israelis support the war with Iran, but many doubt that it will solve Israel’s long-term security problems. Some also question their prime minister’s assurances and motives.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
item.media:description: Residents standing near their building, which was damaged in a missile strike, in Tel Aviv last month.

item.title: Nadav Lapid’s Film ‘Yes’ Is Intended to Anger Both Israel’s Right and Left
item.description: The director Nadav Lapid uses his work to confront his discomfort with nationalism in his home country, never more so than in “Yes.”
item.dc:creator: Esther Zuckerman

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Helene Cooper, Yeganeh Torbati, Adam Rasgon and Ronen Bergman

item.title: In Syria, Kidnappings of Women and Girls Fuel a Minority Group’s Fears
item.description: A Times investigation found that abductions of women and girls from Syria’s Alawite minority were more common, and more brutal, than the government has acknowledged.
item.dc:creator: Ben Hubbard and Laura Boushnak
item.media:description: A 16-year-old girl who said she was kidnapped and raped, and later found out she was pregnant.

item.title: Trump’s Strait of Hormuz Problem
item.description: Our White House reporter Zolan Kanno-Youngs parses some of President Trump’s conflicting messages about the war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs, Christina Thornell, Coleman Lowndes and Whitney Shefte
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Thursday
item.description: President Trump’s prime-time address received a defiant response from a top leader in Tehran.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Amit Elkayam for The New York Times
item.media:description: Workers inspecting the damage from a strike by Iran on Petah Tikva, Israel, on Thursday.

item.title: Macron Voices Europe’s Frustration With Trump’s Handling of Iran War
item.description: President Emmanuel Macron of France expressed disapproval about President Trump’s handling of the war against Iran on Thursday, chastising him for speaking cavalierly in a speech.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler, Erika Solomon and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Emmanuel Macron of France in Seoul on Thursday. Addressing President Trump’s verbal attacks against NATO, he said, “If you create doubt every day about your commitment, you hollow it out.”

item.title: Iranians hold a nature festival in the shadow of war.
item.description: Typically on Sizdah Bedar, families picnic outside, gather around rivers or streams, tell stories and take part in simple traditions that bind them to their heritage.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati and Sanam Mahoozi

item.title: A Bid to Use Force to Open Strait of Hormuz Hits Roadblocks at U.N. Security Council
item.description: Several permanent members of the Security Council opposed the resolution, drafted by Bahrain in coordination with its Gulf neighbors, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi and Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Cargo ships sailing through the Persian Gulf and toward the Strait of Hormuz last month. Traffic through the Strait has slowed drastically since the start of the war.

item.title: Trump Hails Strike on Iranian Bridge, Warning ‘More to Follow’
item.description: Iran state media said eight people had been killed in the attack and dozens wounded. A local official said the bridge, in the mountains, was not yet in operation and that the people had been nearby when the attack happened.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward, Edward Wong, Parin Behrooz and Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump at the Oval Office on Tuesday. Mr. Trump celebrated a strike on a bridge between Tehran and Karaj, Iran, in a social media post on Thursday.

item.title: Iran Says It Will Oversee Strait of Hormuz Traffic, Even in Peacetime
item.description: The United States says Iran’s claims to control the strait are illegal, but Tehran has made escalating assertions of authority over the vital waterway.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A tanker anchored off the coast of Muscat, Oman, last month. At its narrowest, the Strait of Hormuz sits entirely within the territorial waters of Oman and Iran.

item.title: Trump Says He’ll End the Iran War in Weeks. What If He Can’t?
item.description: President Trump faces the possibility that at the end of his own two-to-three week window for wrapping up the war in Iran, nothing much will have changed.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Legal experts express concerns over ‘violations of international law’ in the war.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Taub
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: The aftermath of an airstrike in Tehran, the Iranian capital, on Monday.

item.title: War With Iran Clarifies Trump’s Spending Priorities: The Military, Not Child Care
item.description: As the White House prepares to release its 2027 budget, President Trump said military protection, not social programs, took precedence.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A building in Tehran damaged by airstrikes. This week, President Trump said: “It’s not possible for us to take care of day care, Medicaid, Medicare, all of these individual things, they can do it on a state basis.”

item.title: Mortgage Rates Climb for 5th Week as Iran War Weighs on U.S. Housing Market
item.description: The average rate on a 30-year mortgage in the United States jumped to 6.46 percent, making it harder for buyers to afford homes.
item.dc:creator: Gregory Schmidt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Standard & Poor's Case-Shiller Home Price Index

item.title: Macron Snaps Back at Trump, Reflecting Europe’s Growing Anger Over Iran
item.description: President Emmanuel Macron of France is among several European officials to speak out about the accumulated effect of President Trump’s criticisms of NATO and his war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Emmanuel Macron of France and President Trump meeting during the United Nations General Assembly last year.

item.title: Iranian officials come out defiant after Trump’s war speech.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon and Leily Nikounazar
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: The funeral of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps navy commander in Tehran, on Wednesday.

item.title: Lost Friendships, Broken Relationships: How the War in Iran Is Dividing the Diaspora
item.description: Amid months of protest, repression and war in their native country, Iranians living abroad are navigating their biggest rifts yet.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati and Sanam Mahoozi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Ilvy Njiokiktjien for The New York Times
item.media:description: Osveh Varastegan, who left Iran in 2020, at the gym where she works as a personal trainer in Rotterdam, the Netherlands.

item.title: After Sting Operation, Cousin of Bashar al-Assad Convicted in Arms for Drugs Deal
item.description: The cousin, Antoine Kassis, was found guilty of conspiracy to support a terrorist group, after trying to sell weapons from the fallen regime to a Colombian militia.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.media:description: A defaced billboard of former Syrian leader Bashar al Assad, in Aleppo, last year.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler, Jason Karaian, Erika Solomon and Zolan Kanno-Youngs

item.title: 5 Takeaways From Trump’s Speech on Iran
item.description: President Trump did not define a clear path out of the conflict, which he estimated would end within three weeks.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater and Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: President Trump giving a prime-time address from the White House on Wednesday.

item.title: Transcript: Trump’s Speech on Iran War
item.description: The president made his case for the U.S. attack, and said that the main objectives had been achieved.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Wednesday.
item.description: Formidable strikes rocked Tehran early Wednesday morning. Iran launched what its state-aligned media called one of the largest attacks on Israel yet, damaging several cities.
item.dc:creator: Eve Sampson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: The aftermath of a deadly airstrike in Beirut, Lebanon, on Wednesday.

item.title: In Speech, Trump Is Expected to Call Iran War a Success
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump in the Oval Office on Tuesday. He has given contradictory statements about his goals and endgame for the war.

item.title: Trump Initially Laid Out Five Goals for the Iran War. Here’s Where They Stand.
item.description: The United States and Israel have done significant damage to Iran’s military capabilities. But Iran still fires missiles, has nuclear material and coordinates with militias in the region.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A new mural in Valiasr Square in Tehran on Wednesday. A large number of Iran’s ballistic missiles and launchers remain undamaged after a month of airstrikes by the United States and Israel.
